sentence_jp: 父親は子供を(すくおう)として川に飛びこんだ
sentence_en: The father jumped into the river intending to save his child.
choices: 泳おう、助おう、救おう、守おう
why_correct: 救う(すくう) correctly becomes the volitional 救おう ('trying to save').
why_incorrect: 泳おう, 助おう, 守おう are all incorrectly conjugated forms (the real volitionals would be 泳ごう, 助けよう, 守ろう) and none of them are read すくおう anyway.

sentence_jp: かぜは三日で(なおった)
sentence_en: My cold got better in three days.
choices: 始った、治った、拾った、置った
why_correct: 治る(なおる) = to heal/recover; 治った fits 'got better.'
why_incorrect: 始った and 置った are not valid conjugations (真の forms would be 始まった／置いた). 拾った means 'picked up' — wrong meaning.

sentence_jp: 旅行の(ひよう)は三万円です
sentence_en: The trip costs 30,000 yen.
choices: 費要、貝用、費用、貝要
why_correct: 費用 = cost/expenses, correct word and reading.
why_incorrect: 貝要, 費要, 貝用 all swap in look-alike kanji (貝 for 費, 要 for 用) to form non-existent words.

sentence_jp: 国から友達が来たので、京都を(あんない)した
sentence_en: A friend came from my country, so I showed them around Kyoto.
choices: 案肉、安内、案内、安肉
why_correct: 案内する = to guide/show around; correct word.
why_incorrect: 案肉, 安内, 安肉 swap in similar-sounding/looking kanji (案/安 both read あん; 内/肉 look similar) to make non-words.

sentence_jp: 病気になって、(けんこう)の大切さに気づいた
sentence_en: After getting sick, I realized how important health is.
choices: 建康、健候、健康、建候
why_correct: 健康 = health; correct word.
why_incorrect: 建康, 健候, 建候 swap look-alike kanji (健/建 both けん; 康/候) into non-existent words.

sentence_jp: 本棚を(せいり)してください
sentence_en: Please organize the bookshelf.
choices: 正利、整利、正理、整理
why_correct: 整理する = to organize/tidy; correct word.
why_incorrect: 正利, 整利, 正理 mix up look-alike kanji (正/整 both せい; 利/理 both り) into non-words.

sentence_jp: 来年大学を(そつぎょう)する
sentence_en: I'll graduate university next year.
choices: 率行、率業、卒行、卒業
why_correct: 卒業する = to graduate; correct word.
why_incorrect: 率行, 率業, 卒行 confuse 率(りつ/そつ-like shape) with 卒, and 行 with 業 — none form real words.

sentence_jp: 私は英語が(とくい)だ
sentence_en: I'm good at English.
choices: 特意、特息、得息、得意
why_correct: 得意 = good at/skilled in; correct word.
why_incorrect: 特意, 特息, 得息 mix up look-alike kanji (特/得 both とく; 意/息 look similar) into non-words.

sentence_jp: うそをついた自分を(はんせい)した
sentence_en: I reflected on myself for lying.
choices: 反正、半生、反省、半清
why_correct: 反省する = to reflect on/feel remorse for; correct word and meaning.
why_incorrect: 半生(はんせい) is a real word but means 'half of one's life,' the wrong meaning here. 反正 and 半清 are not real words.

sentence_jp: 私が通っている高校はスポーツが()だ
sentence_en: This high school I attend is big on sports.
choices: 豊か、盛ん、完全、立派
why_correct: 盛ん describes an activity being active/thriving — fits sports being a strong point of the school.
why_incorrect: 豊か (abundant) and 立派 (splendid) don't naturally collocate with スポーツ; 完全 (perfect) is the wrong meaning entirely.

sentence_jp: 海外旅行で、習った英語が()ときは、とてもうれしかった
sentence_en: I was so happy when the English I'd learned got through/was understood on my trip abroad.
choices: 通した、詰めた、抜けた、通じた
why_correct: 通じる = to be understood/get across; fits '(my) English was understood.'
why_incorrect: 通した (pushed/let through), 詰めた (packed/stuffed), 抜けた (came out/slipped out) are all wrong meanings for language being understood.

sentence_jp: 今日までにしなければいけない仕事だったので、昨夜は()した
sentence_en: It was work I had to finish by today, so I stayed up all night.
choices: 徹夜、夜中、速達、快速
why_correct: 徹夜する is the set verb phrase meaning 'to stay up all night.'
why_incorrect: 夜中 (midnight/dead of night) is just a time noun, not a verb meaning 'to stay up.' 速達 (express mail) and 快速 (rapid train) are unrelated words.

sentence_jp: 道で()人が有名な歌手だと、後で気がついた
sentence_en: I later realized the person I passed on the street was a famous singer.
choices: 付き合った、すれちがった、近づけた、乗り越した
why_correct: すれちがう = to pass by each other; fits passing a stranger on the street.
why_incorrect: 付き合った (dated/went out with), 近づけた (brought closer, transitive), 乗り越した (rode past one's stop) don't fit the meaning.

sentence_jp: 時間がありませんので、細かい説明は()させていただきます
sentence_en: Since we don't have time, I'll omit the detailed explanation.
choices: 節約、報告、省略、消費
why_correct: 省略する = to omit/abbreviate; fits skipping the detailed explanation.
why_incorrect: 節約 (save money/resources) doesn't fit 'explanation'; 報告 (report) and 消費 (consume) are wrong meanings.

sentence_jp: 物価高で、生活()は去年の1.5倍になった
sentence_en: Due to inflation, living costs became 1.5 times last year's.
choices: 金、代、料、費
why_correct: 生活費 (living expenses) is the fixed compound; 費 is the correct fee-suffix here.
why_incorrect: 生活代, 生活料, 生活金 are not real words — different fee-suffixes attach to different nouns (e.g. 電気代, 送料), and 'living' only pairs with 費.

sentence_jp: 洗濯したのには()おいてください
sentence_en: Please fold the laundry and leave it (once it's washed).
choices: 折って、切って、たたんで、まいて
why_correct: たたむ (to fold) is used for clothes/futons — fits laundry.
why_incorrect: 折る (fold paper/bend something rigid), 切る (cut), 巻く (roll up) don't fit folding clothes.

sentence_jp: タバコは吸わないほうがいいと思っていっても、仕事が終わると()吸ってしまう
sentence_en: Even though I think I shouldn't smoke, I end up smoking without meaning to once work is over.
choices: ついに、つい、とうとう、なるべく
why_correct: つい = unintentionally/before one realizes it; fits the nuance of smoking despite knowing better.
why_incorrect: ついに and とうとう both mean 'finally, at last' (wrong nuance — this isn't a long-awaited event). なるべく means 'as much as possible,' the opposite direction of meaning.

sentence_jp: 父は60歳まで仕事()の生活をしてきた
sentence_en: My father has lived a life centered on work until age 60.
choices: 注目、中心、全体、中旬
why_correct: 仕事中心の生活 ('a life centered on work') is the fixed pattern.
why_incorrect: 注目 (attention/focus, as in a spotlight) and 全体 (the whole) and 中旬 (mid-month, ちゅうじゅん) don't fit this collocation.

sentence_jp: 妹が起きないように()部屋を出た
sentence_en: I quietly left the room so as not to wake my little sister.
choices: じっと、ただ、ぐっすり、そっと
why_correct: そっと = quietly/gently; fits the manner of leaving without waking someone.
why_incorrect: じっと (motionless/staring fixedly), ぐっすり (soundly — used for sleeping, not moving), ただ (only/merely) don't describe the manner of leaving quietly.

sentence_jp: 油は水に溶けない()がある
sentence_en: Oil has the property of not dissolving in water.
choices: 性格、性質、特別、得意
why_correct: 性質 = the inherent nature/property of a thing; fits describing a physical property of oil.
why_incorrect: 性格 (personality) is only used for people, not substances. 特別 (special) and 得意 (good at) are the wrong meanings.

sentence_jp: 授業中の()が悪いと先生に注意された
sentence_en: I was warned by the teacher for having a bad attitude in class.
choices: 態度、癖、基本、格好
why_correct: 態度が悪い ('bad attitude/behavior') is the set phrase.
why_incorrect: 癖 (habit), 基本 (basics), 格好 (appearance/shape) don't fit being warned by a teacher for behavior.

sentence_jp: 病院に行っているのに、よくならない。（）、病院を変えることにした
sentence_en: I keep going to the hospital, but I'm not getting better. So, I decided to change hospitals.
choices: そのうえ、それから、そして、そこで
why_correct: そこで marks a resulting action taken because of the preceding situation — fits 'so I decided to.'
why_incorrect: そのうえ (moreover, adds information) and それから (after that, sequence) and そして (and) don't carry the cause→resulting-decision logic そこで has.

sentence_jp: 靴が小さくて、（）が痛い
sentence_en: My shoes are small, and my toes hurt.
choices: つま先、足首、ひじ、膝
why_correct: つま先 (toes/tip of the foot) is the body part that would hurt from small shoes.
why_incorrect: 足首 (ankle), ひじ (elbow), 膝 (knee) are all the wrong body part for tight shoes.

sentence_jp: その大学を受けるのは、自分の力を（）みたいからです
sentence_en: The reason I'm applying to that university is because I want to test my own ability.
choices: 試して、信じて、入れて、立って
why_correct: 試す = to test; fits 'testing one's own ability.'
why_incorrect: 信じて (believe), 入れて (put in), 立って (stand) are all the wrong meaning for testing one's ability.

sentence_jp: パスポートは（）このカバンに入れたはずなんですけど。
sentence_en: I'm certain I put my passport in this bag...
choices: 少しも、確かに、うっかり、すっかり
why_correct: 確かに = certainly/for sure; fits the speaker's confident claim paired with はず.
why_incorrect: うっかり (carelessly, implies forgetting — contradicts the certainty of はず), すっかり (completely, wrong nuance), 少しも (not even a little, requires a negative predicate) don't fit.

sentence_jp: 外国旅行は、景色も町のようすも、なんでも()に感じる
sentence_en: On an overseas trip, everything — the scenery, the look of the town — feels fresh/new.
choices: 現代、国際、性格、新鮮
why_correct: 新鮮 = fresh/novel; fits everything feeling new and unfamiliar while traveling abroad.
why_incorrect: 現代 (modern era), 国際 (international), 性格 (personality) are all the wrong meaning.

sentence_jp: かびは暗くて（）が多い所にはえる
sentence_en: Mold grows in places that are dark and have a lot of moisture.
choices: 温度、栄養、梅雨、湿気
why_correct: 湿気 = humidity/moisture; fits the conditions mold thrives in.
why_incorrect: 温度 (temperature) and 栄養 (nutrition) don't naturally pair with 多い in this way. 梅雨 (rainy season) is a specific time period, not a general quality like 'a lot of.'

sentence_jp: 夕べは10時まで仕事で会社に（）いた
sentence_en: Last night I stayed at the office working until 10 o'clock.
choices: 残って、止まって、向かって、やって
why_correct: 残る = to remain/stay behind; fits staying late at the office.
why_incorrect: 止まって (stopped) and 向かって (facing/heading toward) are the wrong meaning; やって doesn't fit grammatically with 会社に.

sentence_jp: 本を読みながら、わからないところに線を（）
sentence_en: While reading the book, I drew a line under the parts I didn't understand.
choices: 足した、消した、引いた、囲んだ
why_correct: 線を引く ('to draw a line') is the set phrase.
why_incorrect: 足した (added) and 消した (erased) are the wrong meaning; 囲んだ (circled) pairs with 丸で囲む, not 線を.

sentence_jp: うっかり降りる駅を（）しまった
sentence_en: I carelessly rode past the station where I was supposed to get off.
choices: 乗り換えて、乗り越して、乗り過ぎて、乗り遅れて
why_correct: 乗り越す = to ride past one's stop; fits missing the right station.
why_incorrect: 乗り換えて (transferred trains) and 乗り遅れて (missed the train/arrived too late to board) describe different situations. 乗り過ぎて is not a standard word.

sentence_jp: この本のおかげで、日本の歴史に対する理解が（）
sentence_en: Thanks to this book, my understanding of Japanese history deepened.
choices: 広まった、強まった、高まった、深まった
why_correct: 理解が深まる ('understanding deepens') is the fixed collocation.
why_incorrect: 広まった (spread — used for rumors/customs), 強まった (strengthened — doesn't naturally pair with 理解), 高まった (rose — fits 関心 'interest' better than 理解) all miss the standard pairing.

sentence_jp: 3月は昼は暖かくても、朝と夜は()ことが多い
sentence_en: In March, even when it's warm in the daytime, mornings and nights are often cold.
choices: 冷える、冷やす、冷める、冷ます
why_correct: 冷える is intransitive, describing temperature dropping on its own — fits mornings/nights getting cold.
why_incorrect: 冷やす and 冷ます are transitive (to cool something down deliberately); 冷める is used for something that was hot cooling down (food, enthusiasm) — wrong nuance for ambient temperature.

sentence_jp: 赤ちゃんの歯が()きた
sentence_en: The baby's teeth have started coming in.
choices: 生まれて、生えて、生きて、生んで
why_correct: 生える is used for hair/teeth/plants growing in — fits teeth coming in.
why_incorrect: 生まれて (was born) is for living beings, not teeth; 生きて (lived) and 生んで (gave birth to, transitive) are the wrong meaning.

sentence_jp: ネクタイが（）いますよ
sentence_en: Your tie is crooked.
choices: 流れて、止まって、曲がって、貼って
why_correct: 曲がる = to be bent/crooked; fits describing a crooked tie.
why_incorrect: 流れて (flowing), 止まって (stopped), 貼って (stuck on) are all the wrong meaning for a tie's appearance.

sentence_jp: 種類はメールでなく、（）してください
sentence_en: Please send the documents by mail, not email.
choices: 書留、郵便、速達、郵送
why_correct: 郵送する = to send by post, the natural verb contrasting with email.
why_incorrect: 書留 (registered mail) and 速達 (express mail) are specific mail types, not the general 'send by post' verb; 郵便 (mail/the postal system) is a noun, not the action of sending.

sentence_jp: ()食べなくてもいいですよ
sentence_en: You don't have to force yourself to eat it.
choices: 無理、無駄、満足、不満
why_correct: 無理に／無理して collocates with '(don't) have to force yourself.'
why_incorrect: 無駄 (waste), 満足 (satisfaction), 不満 (dissatisfaction) are all the wrong meaning here.

sentence_jp: 彼はいつも（）ばかり言っていて、働こうとしない
sentence_en: He's always just complaining and won't try to work.
choices: 問題、意見、文句、質問
why_correct: 文句を言う ('to complain') is the set phrase.
why_incorrect: 問題 (problem), 意見 (opinion), 質問 (question) don't fit the same '～ばかり言う' pattern the way 文句 does.

sentence_jp: 約束を（）ような人は信用できない
sentence_en: You can't trust someone who breaks their promises.
choices: わる、やぶる、こわす、たおす
why_correct: 約束を破る ('to break a promise') is the fixed collocation.
why_incorrect: わる and こわす are used for breaking physical objects; たおす means 'to knock down' — none fit an abstract promise.

sentence_jp: 高校生でアルバイトする人の（）は年々増えている
sentence_en: The proportion of high schoolers who work part-time jobs is increasing year by year.
choices: 比例、割合、比較、割引
why_correct: 割合 = proportion/rate; fits describing what percentage of people do something.
why_incorrect: 比例 (mathematical proportionality) isn't used this way in everyday speech; 比較 (comparison) and 割引 (discount) are the wrong meaning.

sentence_jp: クイズの答えを（）、ハワイに行こう！
sentence_en: Guess the quiz answer correctly and let's go to Hawaii!
choices: 当てて、ためして、決めて、くらべて
why_correct: 答えを当てる ('to guess the answer correctly') is the fixed phrase.
why_incorrect: ためして (try), 決めて (decide), くらべて (compare) don't fit 'guessing correctly.'

sentence_jp: 駅前はおおぜいの人で（）していた
sentence_en: The area in front of the station was crowded with a lot of people.
choices: 集まり、こうふん、こんざつ、感動
why_correct: 混雑する ('to be crowded/congested') fits a busy station area.
why_incorrect: 集まり (gathering) is a noun, not a natural verb here; 興奮 (excitement) and 感動 (being moved) are the wrong meaning.

sentence_jp: 有名な歌手が結婚するという（）が流れている
sentence_en: A rumor is going around that a famous singer is getting married.
choices: 広告、伝言、いたずら、うわさ
why_correct: うわさが流れる ('a rumor circulates') is the fixed collocation.
why_incorrect: 広告 (ad), 伝言 (message), いたずら (prank) are all the wrong meaning for something 'circulating' as gossip.

sentence_jp: 朝、子供を（）のが大変だ
sentence_en: It's a struggle to wake my child up in the morning.
choices: すぎる、起きる、起こす、なくす
why_correct: 起こす is the transitive verb ('to wake someone up'), matching the object marker 子供を.
why_incorrect: 起きる is intransitive ('to wake up' oneself) and can't take 子供を as its object; すぎる and なくす are the wrong meaning.

sentence_jp: テレビを見ていて、いい考えを()
sentence_en: While watching TV, I came up with a good idea.
choices: 思いついた、うつった、思いやった、こえた
why_correct: 考えを思いつく ('to come up with an idea') is the fixed phrase.
why_incorrect: うつった (moved/shifted/was reflected), 思いやった (showed consideration for someone), こえた (exceeded) are all the wrong meaning.

sentence_jp: この問題を（）方法はいくらでもある
sentence_en: There are plenty of ways to resolve this problem.
choices: 現れる、誤解する、解決する、希望する
why_correct: 問題を解決する ('to resolve a problem') is the fixed collocation.
why_incorrect: 現れる (to appear), 誤解する (to misunderstand), 希望する (to hope for) don't fit 'ways to resolve a problem.'

sentence_jp: この薬はよく（）から、飲んでみなさい
sentence_en: This medicine is very effective, so try taking it.
choices: こぼす、当たる、きく、高める
why_correct: 薬がきく (効く, 'medicine is effective') is the fixed collocation.
why_incorrect: こぼす (to spill) and 高める (to raise, transitive) are the wrong meaning; 当たる (to hit/be correct) doesn't fit describing medicine's effectiveness.
